title: Bugfix/allow bind multiple host ports to a single container
body:
Signed-off-by: Mohamed Bayomy [mohamed.farid@motorolasolutions.com](mailto:mohamed.farid@motorolasolutions.com)

When trying to bind 2 ports on host side to a contianer
From podman CLI
```
[root@cmep ~]# podman run --rm -it -p 127.0.0.1:11123:11123/udp -p 127.0.0.1:123:11123/udp 7b7f01697879 /bin/bash
[root@470ff2a41d54 /]#
```

From Podman py 
- Without fix
```
Create container with port arguments ('127.0.0.1', 11123) was successful || Current Value is {'11123/udp': [{'HostIp': '127.0.0.1', 'HostPort': '11123'}]}
Create container with port arguments 11213 was successful || Current Value is {'11123/udp': [{'HostIp': '', 'HostPort': '11213'}]}
Failed port value:[('127.0.0.1', 11123), ('127.0.0.1', 112)] option with error Podman API does not support multiple port bound to a single host port.
Failed port value:[1123, 159] option with error Podman API does not support multiple port bound to a single host port.
```

Test Code:
```
import podman
client = podman.PodmanClient(base_url='unix:///run/podman/podman.sock')
ports = [('127.0.0.1' ,11123), 11213, [('127.0.0.1' ,11123),('127.0.0.1', 112)], [1123, 159]]
for port_option in ports:
	try:
		c1 = client.containers.create(name='test', image='test_image:v1', command=['/bin/bash'], ports={'11123/udp': port_option })
		print(f"Create container with port arguments {port_option} was successful || Current Value is {c1.attrs['HostConfig']['PortBindings']}")
		c1.remove()
	except ValueError as e:
		print(f'Failed port value: {port_option} with error {e}')
```


The error is in `containers_create.py` where in description about valid ports input
```
                The values of the dictionary are the corresponding ports to open on the host,
                which can be either:

                - The port number, as an integer.
                    For example: {'2222/tcp': 3333} will expose port 2222 inside the container
                    as port 3333 on the host.
                - None, to assign a random host port.
                    For example: {'2222/tcp': None}.
                - A tuple of (address, port) if you want to specify the host interface.
                    For example: {'1111/tcp': ('127.0.0.1', 1111)}.
                - A list of integers, if you want to bind multiple host ports to a single container
                    port.
                    For example: {'1111/tcp': [1234, 4567]}.

                    For example: {'9090': 7878, '10932/tcp': '8781',
                                  "8989/tcp": ("127.0.0.1", 9091)}
```

- After Fix
```
Create container with port arguments ('127.0.0.1', 11123) was successful || Current Value is {'11123/udp': [{'HostIp': '127.0.0.1', 'HostPort': '11123'}]}
Create container with port arguments 11213 was successful || Current Value is {'11123/udp': [{'HostIp': '', 'HostPort': '11213'}]}
Create container with port arguments [('127.0.0.1', 11123), ('127.0.0.1', 112)] was successful || Current Value is {'11123/udp': [{'HostIp': '127.0.0.1', 'HostPort': '112'}, {'HostIp': '127.0.0.1', 'HostPort': '11123'}]}
Create container with port arguments [1123, 159] was successful || Current Value is {'11123/udp': [{'HostIp': '', 'HostPort': '159'}, {'HostIp': '', 'HostPort': '1123'}]}
```

testing before changes:
```
tox -e coverage
.....
4 failed, 165 passed, 3 skipped, 1 error in 88.45s (0:01:28)
```

After changes
```
tox -e coverage
.....
4 failed, 166 passed, 3 skipped, 1 error in 123.08s (0:02:03)
```

Done, I squashed all commits into one.
